questions.question_text: What is the time complexity of binary search on a sorted array of n elements?
questions.explanation: Binary search halves the search space each step, giving O(log n).

questions.question_text: Which data structure uses LIFO (Last In First Out) order?
questions.explanation: A stack follows LIFO — the last element pushed is the first popped.

questions.question_text: Which OOP principle allows a subclass to provide a specific implementation of a method already defined in its superclass?
questions.explanation: Method overriding lets a subclass redefine a parent method with its own implementation.

questions.question_text: What does the 'private' access modifier mean in OOP?
questions.option_a: Accessible only within the same package
questions.option_c: Accessible only within the same class
questions.explanation: Private restricts access to within the declaring class only — core to encapsulation.

questions.question_text: What does the 'with' statement do in Python?
questions.option_b: Manages context — opens and auto-closes resources
questions.explanation: 'with' uses context managers to ensure resources like files are properly closed.

questions.question_text: Which of the following creates a generator in Python?
questions.explanation: Using 'yield' instead of 'return' makes a function a generator.

questions.question_text: Which SQL clause is used to filter groups after a GROUP BY?
questions.explanation: HAVING filters aggregated results after GROUP BY; WHERE filters rows before grouping.

questions.question_text: What type of JOIN returns all rows from both tables, with NULLs where there is no match?
questions.explanation: FULL OUTER JOIN returns all rows from both tables, filling NULLs where no match exists.

questions.question_text: In Docker, what is the purpose of a Dockerfile?
questions.option_b: To define instructions for building a Docker image
questions.explanation: A Dockerfile is a script of instructions that Docker uses to build an image.

questions.question_text: Which Git command creates a new branch and switches to it in one step?
questions.explanation: 'git checkout -b' creates and switches to the new branch in one command.
